zh.lifeSummary: 當Juliane de Cornhill於1170年出生在英國諾薩普頓史爾時，她的父親William de Cornhill10歲，母親Juliana26歲。她和Robert Chacombe至少有2個女兒。她去世於1261年在蘇格蘭享年91歲，埋葬在蘇格蘭費伏林多雷斯 艾比。

nameMeanings.name: Cornhill
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of Family Names in Britain and Ireland © University of the West of England 2016
meanings: 1  English: locative name in SE England mainly from Cornhill in London but elsewhere alternatively from Cornhill (Northumb) or one of the many minor places so called. It has often become   Cornell   and has sometimes been confused with   Cornwall  , and   Cornwell  , as in the case of Thomas Cornell, 1722 in   Parish Registers   (Horringer, Suffolk), who is also called Cornwall (1736), Cornwell (1740) and Cornhill (1766).

variants.name: Cornwall
meanings: habitational name from the county of Cornwall, which is named with the Old English tribal name Cornwealas. This is from Kernow (the term that the Cornish used to refer to themselves, a word of uncertain etymology, perhaps connected with a Celtic element meaning ‘horn, headland’), + Old English wealas ‘strangers, foreigners’, the term used by the Anglo-Saxons for British-speaking people.

variants.name: Parish Registers
meanings: English:  see  Lonsdale  .   Redmonds, Dictionary of Yorks Surnames   notes Tobias Londsdale or Lansdall, 1675–84 in   Parish Registers   (Keighley, WR Yorks).

variants.name: Cornwell
meanings: habitational name from Cornwell in Oxfordshire, named from Old English corn, a metathesized form of cron, cran ‘crane’ + well(a) ‘spring, stream’.

variant of Cornwall or Cornhill; in southeastern England mainly a habitational name from Cornhill in London but elsewhere alternatively from Cornhill (Northumberland) or one of the many minor places so called, often becoming Cornell .

variants.name: Cornell
meanings: English: shortened form of Cornwell , Cornwall , or of Cornhill, a habitational name from a place in Northumberland named Cornhill, from Old English corn, a metathesized form of cron, cran ‘crane’ + halh ‘nook, recess’; or from Cornhill in London, a medieval grain exchange, named with Old English corn ‘corn, grain’ + hyll ‘hill’, or from some other place elsewhere similarly named.
English: possibly also a nickname from French corneille ‘rook, crow’, probably denoting a chatterer or someone with dark hair or a dark complexion.

variants.name: Cornall
meanings: 1 English: the Lancs name is a variant of Corney , where -all and -ell are hypercorrect pronunciations of Cornah, Corner, and Cornoe, late pronunciations of Corney.

2 English: the Devon name is a variant of Cornwall . Here also probably belongs Gurnall, as in Joan Gurnall, 1586 in IGI (Crediton, Devon), and William Gurnall, 1650 in IGI (Lavenham, Suffolk). Compare Corney .

nameMeanings.name: Juliane
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Modern combination of the given names   Julie   and   Anne  , perhaps sometimes regarded as a feminine form of   Julian .

variants.name: Julieann
meanings: Modern combination of the given names   Julie   and   Anne  , perhaps sometimes regarded as a feminine form of   Julian .

variants.name: Julieanne
meanings: Modern combination of the given names   Julie   and   Anne  , perhaps sometimes regarded as a feminine form of   Julian .

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.

historicEvents.title: Magna Carta
historicEvents.description: King John of England agreed to a charter of rights called the Magna Carta. The document was originally drafted by the Archbishop of Canterbury to establish peace between King John and a group of rebel barons. The Magna Carta was completed in 1215 and is now considered to be one of the greatest constitutional documents of all time.

historicEvents.title: France Declares War on England
historicEvents.description: France declared war on England as Louis VIII of France attacked Poitou and northern Gascony.

historicEvents.title: 100 Years of Conflict Between England and France Ends
historicEvents.description: Treaty of Paris was signed on December 4, 1259, which ended 100 years of conflict between England and France.

en.lifeSummary: When Juliane de Cornhill was born in 1170, in Northamptonshire, England, her father, William de Cornhill, was 10 and her mother, Juliana, was 26. She had at least 2 daughters with Robert Chacombe. She died in 1261, in Scotland, at the age of 91, and was buried in Lindores Abbey, Fife, Scotland.

Elle a eu au moins 2 filles avec Robert Chacombe. Elle est décédée en 1261, à Écosse, à l’âge de 91 ans, et a été enterrée à Lindores Abbey, Fife, Écosse.

Sie hatte mit Robert Chacombe mindestens 2 Töchter. Sie starb 1261 im Alter von 91 Jahren in Scotland und wurde in Lindores Abbey, Fife, Scotland begraben.

ja.lifeSummary: Juliane de Cornhillが1170年にイギリスノーサンプトンシュアイアで生まれた時，父William de Cornhillは10歳，母Julianaは26歳であった。彼女はRobert Chacombeとの間に少なくとも2人の娘をもうけた。彼女は1261年にスコットランドで91歳で死去し，スコットランドフィフリンドアス アビーにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Juliane de Cornhill(이)가 1170년 영국 노르섬프토실르에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 William de Cornhill(은)는 10세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Juliana(은)는 26세였다. 그녀는 Robert Chacombe(와)과 적어도 두 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1261년 스컷랜드에서 91세의 나이로 사망했으며 스컷랜드 피프 린도어스 애브브이에 묻혔다.

ru.Cornhill on Tweed, Northumberland, Engla: корнилл он твид, нортумбирланд, Англия, Великобритания
ru.lifeSummary: Когда Juliane de Cornhill родилась в 1170 г. в местности нортамптоншир, Англия, её отцу по имени William de Cornhill было 10 лет, а матери, которую звали Juliana, 26. У неё было по крайней мере 2 дочери от Robert Chacombe. Она умерла в 1261 г. в местности Шотландия в возрасте 91 год и была похоронена в местности линдоресь эбби, фиф, Шотландия.
